qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Repository-controlled matrix filter reaches GroovyShell evaluation on the Jenkins controller
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an expression-injection path in LifecycleGenerator.matrixExcludeFilterBuilder and MultiPlatformGenerator.getBuildableMatrixAxes, while the advisory is a SHA-256 hex-padding defect in SecurityIO.sha256Sum. They do not share the vulnerable function, sink, or root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SecurityIO.sha256Sum padding bug or an equivalent hash-comparison root cause.
evidence: The advisory identifies SecurityIO.sha256Sum in src/main/groovy/net/gleske/jervis/tools/SecurityIO.groovy, where SHA-256 output was padded with padLeft(32, '0') instead of padLeft(64, '0').
The only final qualifying finding in .judge/input_manifest.json is matrix_filter_groovyshell_rce.

That finding's reported code locations are prepareJervisLifecycleGenerator.groovy, LifecycleGenerator.groovy, and MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy, culminating in GroovyShell.evaluate(generator.matrixExcludeFilter()).
The qualifying finding describes controller-side Groovy injection from .jervis.yml matrix filters, not a truncated SHA-256 hex string or hash-comparison failure in SecurityIO.
